title: Diagonalization Algorithm
description: Algorithm to find P and D for a diagonalizable matrix
code:
INPUT: Matrix A of size n×n
OUTPUT: Matrix P (eigenvectors) and D (eigenvalues)

BEGIN
1. Find characteristic polynomial: det(A - λI)
2. Solve det(A - λI) = 0 for eigenvalues λ₁,λ₂,...,λₙ
3. FOR each eigenvalue λᵢ:
 a. Solve (A - λᵢI)v = 0
 b. Find basis vectors for null space
 c. Store eigenvectors as columns in P
4. Form diagonal matrix D with eigenvalues
5. Verify P is invertible (det(P) ≠ 0)
6. OUTPUT P and D
END
explanation: Step-by-step procedure: find eigenvalues from characteristic equation, find eigenvectors by solving (A-λI)v=0, form matrices P and D

title: Power of Matrix Using Diagonalization
description: Efficient computation of matrix powers using diagonalization
code:
INPUT: Matrix A (diagonalizable), power n
OUTPUT: Aⁿ

BEGIN
1. DIAGONALIZE: Find P and D such that A = PDP⁻¹
2. COMPUTE Dⁿ: For diagonal D, Dⁿ[i][i] = (D[i][i])^n
3. COMPUTE Aⁿ = P × Dⁿ × P⁻¹
4. OUTPUT Aⁿ

EXAMPLE for A = [[2,0],[0,3]]:
- D = [[2,0],[0,3]] (already diagonal)
- D¹⁰ = [[2¹⁰, 0],[0, 3¹⁰]]
- A¹⁰ = [[1024, 0],[0, 59049]]
END
explanation: Diagonal matrices are easy to raise to powers - just raise each diagonal element. This simplifies otherwise complex matrix multiplication

complexity.time: O(n³) for diagonalization + O(n²) for power

title: Orthogonal Diagonalization of Symmetric Matrix
description: Finding orthogonal matrix Q to diagonalize symmetric matrix
code:
INPUT: Symmetric matrix A
OUTPUT: Orthogonal Q and diagonal D

BEGIN
1. VERIFY symmetry: Check A = Aᵀ
2. Find eigenvalues λ₁,λ₂,...,λₙ of A
3. For each λᵢ, find orthonormal eigenvectors:
 a. Solve (A - λᵢI)v = 0
 b. Use Gram-Schmidt to orthonormalize
4. Form Q from normalized eigenvectors as columns
5. Verify QᵀQ = I (orthogonal property)
6. Compute D = QᵀAQ
7. OUTPUT Q and D

PROPERTY: Q⁻¹ = Qᵀ simplifies computation
END
explanation: For symmetric matrices, eigenvectors can be made orthonormal, and Q⁻¹ = Qᵀ makes the diagonalization computationally simpler
